title: Design outdoor dry-bulb and wet-bulb temperatures
summary: Design outdoor dry-bulb and wet-bulb temperatures (absolute and coincident) should be included in HVAC&R system program.
evidence_quote: design outdoor dry-bulb and wet-bulb temperatures (absolute and coincident)

title: Ventilation rate minimum for gas removal spaces
summary: Ventilation rate in spaces with gas removal provisions should be maintained at no less than 15 cfm per person.
evidence_quote: the ventilation rate in these types of spaces should be maintained at no less than 15 cfm [7.1 L/s] per person

title: Maximum air movement for thermal comfort
summary: Standard 55 prescribes maximum air speed of 30 fpm for winter and 50 fpm for summer.
evidence_quote: maximum rate of air movement (air speed) of 30 fpm [0.15 m/s] for winter and 50 fpm [0.25 m/s] for summer

title: ADPI prediction applicability temperature
summary: ADPI predictions apply only if average space temperature is close to 76°F.
evidence_quote: ADPI predictions apply only if the average space temperature is close to that value [76°F]

title: Quick-opening valve application
summary: Quick-opening characteristic is applied where process requires essentially ON/OFF operation, e.g., preheat coil in outdoor air duct.
evidence_quote: The quick-opening characteristic is usually applied where a process requires essentially ON/OFF operation.

title: Linear valve application on steam coil
summary: Linear valve is typically applied on a steam-heating coil with discharge sensor.
evidence_quote: A typical application would be on a steam-heating coil operated by a controller whose sensor is on the discharge side of the coil.

title: Equal-percentage valve for hydronic coil
summary: Equal-percentage valve is used for hydronic heating or cooling coil to counteract nonlinear coil heat transfer.
evidence_quote: An example is the control of a hydronic heating or cooling coil.

title: Valve coefficient Cv definition
summary: Cv coefficient is flow in gpm through a wide-open valve at 1 psi pressure drop.
evidence_quote: the Cv coefficient is the flow (in gpm) through a wide-open valve at a 1 psi pressure drop across the valve

title: Steam flow formula through valve
summary: For steam, Ws = 2.1 Cv [(p1 + p2)(p1 - p2)]^0.5.

title: Thermostat location guidelines
summary: Room thermostats should not be on exterior walls, dead corners, or unsuitable locations; best locations provide air motion and sense average temperature.
evidence_quote: Room thermostats should not be located on exterior walls, in dead corners, or in other unsuitable locations

title: Security enclosure airflow requirement
summary: Metal or plastic box-type security enclosures with minimal air slits should not be used as they restrict airflow.
evidence_quote: Metal or plastic box-type security enclosures with minimal air slits should not be used

title: Thermostat on exterior wall insulation
summary: If thermostats must be on exterior walls, insulating bases should be used and wall penetration sealed airtight.
evidence_quote: If thermostats must be installed on exterior walls, insulating bases should be used and the wall penetration sealed airtight.

title: Slider bar temperature adjustment
summary: Slider bar can be programmed to establish how much temperature adjustment is allowed.
evidence_quote: The slider bar can be programmed to establish how much temperature adjustment is allowed.

title: Single-zone constant-volume control
summary: Very small single-zone air-handling units can be controlled by simple two-position electric or electronic thermostat/valve systems.
evidence_quote: Very small single-zone air-handling units ... can be controlled by simple two-position electric or electronic thermostat/valve systems
